root_cause.text: The `doFillGoogleCredentialsIdItems` and `doCheckGoogleCredentialsId` methods in `GooglePlayBuildStepDescriptor.java` lack proper permission checks, allowing unauthorized users to enumerate and validate Google OAuth credential IDs.
root_cause.evidence: The patch introduces `if (item == null || !item.hasPermission(EXTENDED_READ))` checks in both methods, along with the comment `// Only allow enumerating credentials if we have the appropriate permission`.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `doFillGoogleCredentialsIdItems` and `doCheckGoogleCredentialsId` methods in `GooglePlayBuildStepDescriptor.java` lack proper permission checks, allowing unauthorized users to enumerate and validate Google OAuth credential IDs.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch introduces `if (item == null || !item.hasPermission(EXTENDED_READ))` checks in both methods, along with the comment `// Only allow enumerating credentials if we have the appropriate permission`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update imports to support permission checks for credential retrieval.
intent_record.what: Added and removed several Java imports related to credentials, Jenkins items, and Stapler routing.
intent_record.how: Provides the necessary class and constant references to implement permission-aware credential listing and validation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add permission checks to credential filling and checking methods to secure access.
intent_record.what: Added an Item parameter and EXTENDED_READ permission checks to credential enumeration and validation methods.
intent_record.how: Prevents unauthorized enumeration and validation of Google credentials by requiring the user to have appropriate permissions.

grouping_result.intent: Update imports to support permission checks for credential retrieval.
Add permission checks to credential filling and checking methods to secure access.

relevance_review.reason: Updating imports to support permission checks is a necessary step in addressing the lack of permission checks in the credential-related methods, directly tying to the vulnerability fix.
The group directly addresses the vulnerability by adding the missing permission checks to the credential filling and checking methods.

candidates.rationale: This method lists credentials without checking if the current user has permission.
This method validates credentials without checking if the current user has permission.

vuln_statements.explanation: This method lacks permission checks, allowing unauthorized users to enumerate Google OAuth credential IDs.
This method lacks permission checks, allowing unauthorized users to validate Google OAuth credential IDs.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Refactor duplicate code, and add token-macro support.
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit ca45adbe672224e24bef9cc216e9fbf04a6d118d
Author: Christopher Orr <chris@orr.me.uk>
Date:   Thu Jul 30 00:01:13 2015 +0200

    Refactor duplicate code, and add token-macro support.

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/jenkinsci/plugins/googleplayandroidpublisher/GooglePlayBuildStepDescriptor.java b/src/main/java/org/jenkinsci/plugins/googleplayandroidpublisher/GooglePlayBuildStepDescriptor.java
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..dbc4a29
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/main/java/org/jenkinsci/plugins/
bic_trace_step.reason: The methods doFillGoogleCredentialsIdItems and doCheckGoogleCredentialsId are newly introduced in this commit but lack any permission checks (such as checking Jenkins.ADMINISTER or Item.CONFIGURE). This allows unauthorized users to enumerate or validate credentials, exactly matching the vulnerability description.
